articles.title: Andhra Pradesh Braces for Intensifying April Heat - The Hans India
articles.full_text:
Andhra Pradesh Braces for Intensifying April Heat
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued its latest weather update, warning that heat conditions across Andhra Pradesh are set to intensify significantly during April. According to the report, temperatures in the Rayalaseema and Coastal Andhra regions are expected to rise above normal levels.
Alongside the soaring heat, the IMD has indicated the likelihood of intermittent moderate rainfall during the month. Changing weather patterns may bring evening thunderstorms and scattered showers, offering brief relief from the otherwise harsh conditions.
The state is already experiencing extreme heat, with maximum temperatures crossing 40°C in several areas—levels typically associated with May. In the north coastal districts of Visakhapatnam, Vizianagaram and Srikakulam, increasing humidity is contributing to oppressive and sultry weather. These conditions are expected to persist throughout April. Similarly, the twin Godavari districts are also likely to witness elevated temperatures.
In the Rayalaseema region, including Chittoor, Kadapa and Nellore, daytime temperatures are forecast to rise by 2 to 4°C above normal. Nandyal district recorded the highest temperature in the state recently at 40.9°C.
The IMD has further noted that neighbouring Telangana will also face intense heat throughout April. While occasional evening showers are currently providing some respite, the overall trend points towards rising temperatures in the coming weeks.
